purpose: Ground-truth smoke tests for deterministic Ask Data and dashboard behavior. Use Playwright for UI checks and DuckDB/MotherDuck MCP SQL for numerical cross-checks.

tests.duckdbSql: SELECT SUM(s.Sales) AS total_sales FROM read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/sales.csv') s JOIN read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/customer.csv') c USING("Customer ID") WHERE c.Region = 'West'

tests.question: sales by region
tests.duckdbSql: SELECT c.Region AS label, SUM(s.Sales) AS value FROM read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/sales.csv') s JOIN read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/customer.csv') c USING("Customer ID") GROUP BY c.Region ORDER BY value DESC

tests.question: compare sales in west vs east
tests.duckdbSql: SELECT c.Region AS label, SUM(s.Sales) AS value FROM read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/sales.csv') s JOIN read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/customer.csv') c USING("Customer ID") WHERE c.Region IN ('West','East') GROUP BY c.Region ORDER BY value DESC

tests.question: sales for furniture vs technology
tests.duckdbSql: SELECT p.Category AS label, SUM(s.Sales) AS value FROM read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/sales.csv') s JOIN read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/product.csv') p USING("Product ID") WHERE p.Category IN ('Furniture','Technology') GROUP BY p.Category ORDER BY value DESC

tests.question: revenue by customer type
tests.duckdbSql: SELECT c.Segment AS label, SUM(s.Sales) AS value FROM read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/sales.csv') s JOIN read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/customer.csv') c USING("Customer ID") GROUP BY c.Segment ORDER BY value DESC

tests.question: sales by region in offce supplies

warningsContain: Some matches were fuzzy or inferred

tests.duckdbSql: SELECT c.Region AS label, SUM(s.Sales) AS value FROM read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/sales.csv') s JOIN read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/customer.csv') c USING("Customer ID") JOIN read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/product.csv') p USING("Product ID") WHERE p.Category = 'Office Supplies' GROUP BY c.Region ORDER BY value DESC

tests.question: sales over time
tests.duckdbSql: SELECT CAST(period AS VARCHAR) AS label, value FROM (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', s."Order Date") AS period, SUM(s.Sales) AS value FROM read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/sales.csv') s GROUP BY 1) q ORDER BY label ASC LIMIT 3

tests.question: sales change from 2016 to 2017
tests.duckdbSql: WITH yearly AS (SELECT EXTRACT(year FROM s."Order Date") AS yr, SUM(s.Sales) AS value FROM read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/sales.csv') s GROUP BY 1), picked AS (SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN yr=2016 THEN value END) AS start_value, SUM(CASE WHEN yr=2017 THEN value END) AS end_value FROM yearly) SELECT '2016 to 2017' AS period, start_value, end_value, end_value-start_value AS change, (end_value-start_value)/start_value AS change_percent FROM picked

tests.question: share of sales by category
tests.duckdbSql: WITH grouped AS (SELECT p.Category AS label, SUM(s.Sales) AS value FROM read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/sales.csv') s JOIN read_csv_auto('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chinmoy2306/superstore_sales_analysis/refs/heads/main/product.csv') p USING("Product ID") GROUP BY p.Category) SELECT label, value, value / SUM(value) OVER () AS share FROM grouped ORDER BY value DESC
